behaviors.description: The speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so that the summary can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: Real failure is not just field plumbing; it includes diarization quality from the real speech model (speaker label assignment/preservation can vary by audio and model behavior). A stub STT response would hide this. I considered Workflow, but rejected it because this behavior targets a single model step boundary (STT speaker labeling), not a multi-agentic chain requirement.
behaviors.test_description: Run a real STT call on a recorded multi-speaker meeting clip with known speaker turns, then assert transcript segments include speaker labels and that labels are usable downstream for attendee attribution mapping.

behaviors.description: The structured summary contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real LLM extraction quality (detecting assignments and mapping them to attendees). Deterministic mocks would usually pass and miss omissions. I considered Workflow, but rejected it because the behavior can be validated at the single summarization output quality boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Provide a real transcript with explicit task assignments to multiple attendees, call the production summarization model, and assert each attendee with an assignment appears in action items at least once.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the "decisions" section of the summary, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: This is a model judgment/formatting quality check: identifying decisions and placing them in the correct structured section. Stubbed outputs hide this failure. I considered Workflow, but rejected it because the behavior is satisfied by validating one summarization step’s output structure and content placement.
behaviors.test_description: Use transcripts with clearly stated decisions, run the real summarization model, and assert each known decision is present in the structured `decisions` field and not only in freeform narrative text.

behaviors.description: When the audio file is longer than 60 minutes, the service splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: Failure can emerge across multiple dependent agentic steps: chunking, per-segment transcription/summarization, then merge coherence. Later correctness depends on earlier model outputs and sequencing. I considered Agentic, but rejected it because this behavior explicitly requires multi-step sequential processing and merged end result.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real pipeline on a >60-minute recording, verify segment boundaries and sequential execution order, then validate final merged summary preserves cross-segment continuity (no dropped sections, consistent attendees/actions/decisions).

behaviors.description: The full pipeline — upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output — completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: This is explicitly end-to-end and includes multiple real agentic stages (STT + LLM summarization) plus orchestration/delivery. Real failures arise from stage interactions and cumulative model behavior. I considered System, but rejected it because behavior text requires the full multi-step agent pipeline, not only deterministic infrastructure interaction.
behaviors.test_description: Execute a production-like run with a real meeting audio file through upload, transcription, summarization, and output delivery; assert successful completion, no manual retries, and valid structured output schema with populated action items/decisions/attributions.
